mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates directly increase the discount rate for equity valuations, compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month. Higher policy rates reduce corporate borrowing capacity and lower present values of future earnings.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant negative correlation with FEDFUNDS changes (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent, labor market slack triggers immediate profit margin compression and consumer spending contraction, driving negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 65 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0 percent, or the signal produces a negative information coefficient over any 24-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals robust aggregate demand that outpaces supply constraints, forcing equity prices higher over the next month as earnings revisions turn positive.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate a positive Rank IC greater than 0.04 over rolling 3-year windows, or S&P 500 returns are negative in more than 55 percent of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth accelerates during late-cycle expansions, indicating capacity utilization peaks that precede corporate earnings inflection points, generating positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 60-day S&P 500 returns during late-cycle phases, with a walk-forward Sharpe ratio below 0.5 or a negative mean return difference relative to baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation exceeding 3.0 percent forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy stances, which suppresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in over 60 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL remains above 315, or the threshold signal yields a Rank IC below 0.01 over any 36-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with falling unemployment rates creates a tight labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer confidence, triggering immediate positive S&P 500 momentum over one week.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal generates a negative information coefficient over rolling 2-year periods, or 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 58 percent of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Gross domestic product growth directly correlates with corporate revenue expansion, establishing a persistent positive relationship between quarterly economic output and S&P 500 returns over three months.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant positive correlation with 60-day S&P 500 returns (p > 0.05), or the signal produces a negative cumulative alpha over any 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production contractions during post-shock recovery phases indicate temporary supply bottlenecks that resolve quickly, creating mean-reversion opportunities that drive positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns remain negative in over 65 percent of instances where INDPRO falls below 100 during recovery phases, or the mean-reversion signal fails to achieve a positive Rank IC.
